items.title: Counties face $52M in food stamp costs after NC budget omits funding request
items.summary_raw: Counties are absorbing higher administrative costs tied to the food stamp program as federal reimbursements drop. State lawmakers left additional funding out of their newly approved budget.

items.title: NC Zoo historic marker honors Astrochinp Ham
items.summary_raw: A new North Carolina highway historical marker near the N.C. Zoo honors Ham, the first chimpanzee in space, and recognizes his pioneering 1961 mission and final years living with other chimpanzees in Asheboro.

items.title: The UK Is Planning a Social Media Curfew for 16- and 17-Year-Olds
items.summary_raw: The restrictions, which can be turned off, will include a crackdown on “addictive” app features and will be in addition to a total ban on children under 16 accessing platforms like TikTok and YouTube.

items.title: Gov. Hochul orders a pause on data centers in New York for up to a year
items.summary_raw: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an order pausing construction of large data centers — the first statewide pause in the country.

items.title: Spain sparkles to shock France 2-0 and advance to the World Cup final
items.summary_raw: Spain is back in the men's World Cup final for the first time since 2010. That's also when Spain won its only World Cup title. France had hoped to reach the final for the third straight tournament.

items.title: World Cup favorites France unravel as Spain book s...
items.summary_raw: Spain have sealed their place in the 2026 World Cup final, but why did France collapse so easily?

items.title: From Wimbledon towels to Scotch: What India-UK trade deal could mean for shoppers
items.summary_raw: The free trade agreement between the world's fifth and sixth largest economies has come into effect - can it deliver?

items.title: Eating chili peppers may raise the risk of one deadly cancer
items.summary_raw: A major review found that people who consumed the most chili peppers had a substantially higher risk of esophageal cancer, though the evidence was less clear for stomach and colorectal cancers. Researchers emphasize that the findings show an association, not proof of cause and effect, and that more research is needed to determine whether moderate consumption carries similar risks.

items.title: Man gets life in prison for killing girlfriend, trying to kill her son in Johnston County
items.summary_raw: A man accused of killing his girlfriend and shooting her son inside a Johnston County home in March will spend the rest of his life in prison.

items.title: Drought conditions make hot weather even hotter
items.summary_raw: While the lack of rainfall is an obvious part of North Carolina's ongoing drought, there's another side effect that many people don't realize. Drought conditions can make hot weather even hotter.

items.title: YouTube and X Have Become ‘Gateways’ to Nudify Apps
items.summary_raw: A new study found that social media platforms are referring people to sites where they can create nonconsensual, sexually explicit deepfakes for as little as $1 an image.

items.title: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
items.summary_raw: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will pause non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in less than a week, Maine U.S. Sen. Angus King's office tells NPR.

items.title: Questions continue about ICE shooting in Maine
items.summary_raw: Investigations are underway in Southern Maine, one day after a fatal shooting by an ICE agent. The agency says it was seeking a different person. Protests and vigils continue.

items.title: France-Spain player ratings: Porro, Rodri earn hig...
items.summary_raw: Spain received strong performances up and down the lineup as they beat France to make the World Cup final.

items.title: Watch: The clash between US and Iran for control of the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_raw: The BBC's Barbara Plett Usher examines the latest tensions over the key international shipping channel.

items.title: Are there aliens on exoplanet K2-18b? Scientists just scanned it for signals
items.summary_raw: K2-18b is one of the most promising worlds for the search for extraterrestrial life, so astronomers conducted an unusually powerful radio survey using both the VLA and MeerKAT telescopes. Advanced software analyzed millions of signals, filtering out Earth-based interference and other false positives. No convincing artificial radio transmissions were found, but the project demonstrated a powerful new approach that will make future SETI searches faster and far more effective.

items.title: Truck crash closes several lanes on I-95 near Kenly
items.summary_raw: Sky 5 flew over I-95 near the Kenly area and saw several first responders as well as a truck carrying construction equipment that had overturned and was against a guard rail.

items.title: New York Governor Signs First Statewide Data Center Moratorium
items.summary_raw: “We have no choice but to address the challenges created by these massive facilities,” New York governor Kathy Hochul said. The executive order will pause construction for one year.

items.title: Investigative journalist reports on the abuse inside ICE's largest detention facility
items.summary_raw: The New Yorker writer Jonathan Blitzer says thousands of people are being held in tents in the El Paso desert, where inhumane conditions have become a tool to pressure people to accept deportation.

items.title: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
items.summary_raw: What's it like to grow up and learn in the age of AI? NPR put that question to seven teenagers across the country.

items.title: Copy of Why England vs. Argentina is World Cup's best, ang...
items.summary_raw: Wednesday will see England and Argentina meet for the first time since 2005, and it's a rivalry that has defined the World Cup thanks to some unforgettable encounters. Here's why this game is so compelling.

items.title: Wife describes moment husband nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane
items.summary_raw: A man was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window mid-air on a Ryanair plane.

items.title: A 37-year soil experiment revealed a hidden climate threat
items.summary_raw: The world's longest-running soil warming experiment has revealed an unexpected climate concern. After nearly four decades, researchers found that warming can cause microbes to break down stable soil carbon that scientists once believed was largely protected. That releases extra carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, potentially accelerating global warming.

items.title: 'It's not worth it:' Mother of murder victim disavows retaliatory shooting outside Wilson courthouse
items.summary_raw: Kimberly Simmons was inside the courthouse to follow the court proceedings in her son’s murder when shots rang out. The sheriff says that shooting was in part to avenge LaDarrius Simmons' death.

items.title: Plex Keeps Getting Worse. Is Jellyfin a Decent Replacement?
items.summary_raw: If you want to stream local media, this free and open source media server is just as good as Plex. But if you rely on remote access or live TV, prepare to tinker.

items.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
items.summary_raw: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.

items.title: Former immigration official assesses the tactics behind Trump's immigration crackdown
items.summary_raw: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Gil Kerlikowske, commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection under President Obama, about the recent string of fatal shootings by federal immigration agents.

items.title: How Argentina coach Scaloni unlocked Messi's goal-...
items.summary_raw: Lionel Messi went from six goals in four World Cups to 15 (and counting) in 2022 and 2026.

items.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
items.summary_raw: Trump drops a 24-hour-old vow to charge cargo ships for using the Strait as the US continues its battle to break Iran's hold on the waterway.

items.title: Popular weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy may slow biological aging
items.summary_raw: Researchers found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, slowed biological aging markers in adults with HIV, marking the first clinical evidence that the drug may influence human aging. Although the findings are encouraging, scientists say larger studies are needed before concluding that the medication can help people age more slowly.

items.title: Utilities sought record rate increases this spring as Duke's request remains before NC regulators
items.summary_raw: A new report found utilities sought a record-high $9.2 billion in rate increases during the second quarter. North Carolina accounts for nearly $900 million of those requests, including Duke Energy's filings.

items.title: Best RGB TVs (2026): My Picks After Testing the Hottest TVs
items.summary_raw: RGB TVs are the latest hotness in the world of televisions, and I’ve tested many of the latest models to see which you should buy.

items.title: South Carolina's governor appoints Lindsey Graham's sister to serve out his term
items.summary_raw: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster says he is appointing Darline Graham to finish the term of her late brother, former Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died in office Saturday at age 71.

items.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
items.summary_raw: The payment comes three years after a jury found President Trump liable for sexually abusing and defaming the writer.

items.summary_raw: Svetlana Grković told Serbian media that her husband is "seriously injured and in shock".

items.title: This dinosaur fossil captures the final moments of a T. rex attack
items.summary_raw: A fossilized Edmontosaurus skull with a Tyrannosaurus tooth still embedded in its face has given scientists rare evidence of a dramatic predator-prey encounter. The discovery suggests the giant carnivore delivered an incredibly powerful face-to-face bite, offering new clues about how Tyrannosaurus hunted.

items.title: Durham father charged with shooting his son during family dispute on Burgundy Road
items.summary_raw: Deputies say a Durham father is charged after allegedly shooting his 27-year-old son during an argument Tuesday morning. The son suffered serious but non-life-threatening injuries.

items.summary_raw: There’s more to Google’s messaging app than you might realize.

items.title: Some states will ask voters to make it harder to pass constitutional amendments
items.summary_raw: Several states have ballot measures this year that could raise the thresholds needed to pass state constitutional amendments. Many advocates are critical of such limits on direct democracy.

items.summary_raw: Control of the waterway has become a key point of contention between the US and Iran, as a ceasefire deal falters.

items.title: NASA's Perseverance just completed a marathon on Mars
items.summary_raw: NASA's Perseverance rover has reached an impressive new milestone on Mars, completing the equivalent of a full marathon by driving 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers) across the Red Planet. It accomplished the feat in just five years and four months, reaching the distance on its 1,890th Martian day, less than half the time it took the previous record holder, NASA's Opportunity rover.

items.title: Fact-check: Did Mitch McConnell's statement about his health include a fake photo?
items.summary_raw: Social media users spread conspiracy theories about Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., following a statement he released on July 12.

items.title: Oura Ring 5 Review: Still the Smart Ring to Beat
items.summary_raw: It’s not the upgrade Ring 4 owners have been waiting for, but it’s easily the best smart ring Oura has ever made.

items.title: Platner's voters are reeling as Maine Democratic Party races to choose his replacement
items.summary_raw: Graham Platner generated enthusiasm in Maine with his progressive policies. Now he's out of the race, and his supporters have to decide what's next, as Maine Democrats prepare to choose a nominee.

items.title: Family of a man shot by agents responding to a 911 call seeks answers
items.summary_raw: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man shot by agents told NPR they want answers.

items.title: Projecting the USMNT 2030 World Cup roster
items.summary_raw: The tears are barely dry from the USMNT's 2026 World Cup exit, but the page immediately turns to the 2030 World Cup. Here's who could represent the U.S. in four years.

items.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
items.summary_raw: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

items.title: NASA selects four new Moon missions to build a permanent lunar base
items.summary_raw: NASA is ramping up its lunar ambitions by awarding nearly $600 million for four commercial Moon landings planned for late 2028. Each mission will carry the same trio of science instruments to improve lunar navigation, study dangerous dust kicked up during landings, and map the Moon's radiation environment. The agency also revealed plans for new rovers, communication satellites, and additional cargo missions as it lays the groundwork for a permanent Moon Base.

items.title: House damaged in fire on Sugarberry Lane in Clayton, no injuries reported
items.summary_raw: Firefighters brought a house fire on Sugarberry Lane under control in about 20 minutes. No injuries were reported.

items.title: This German Man Is on a Quest to Cut the Perfect Slice of Bread
items.summary_raw: Armed with high-end knives and digital calipers, Germanbreadcutter has entranced thousands of fans, one loaf at a time.

items.title: Trump's National Guard deployment in D.C. has been extended until 2029
items.summary_raw: President Trump's deployment of the National Guard to Washington, D.C., has been extended several times. Now it's set to last until Inauguration Day 2029.

items.title: Maine Sen. Angus King talks about fatal shooting of a man by federal agents
items.summary_raw: Sen. Angus King, I-Maine, reacts to the fatal shooting of a 26-year-old Colombian man by federal law enforcement in Biddeford, Maine, Monday morning.

items.title: WNBA Power Rankings: The Fever are running hot
items.summary_raw: With Kelsey Mitchell posting her sixth straight game with 25 points or more, Indiana moves up to No. 4. Golden State holds on to the top spot.

items.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
items.summary_raw: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

items.title: Scientists discover why peach fuzz can suddenly make you itch
items.summary_raw: A newly discovered network of fine hairs and specialized nerve cells appears to form a dedicated system for sensing mechanical itch, offering fresh insight into why chronic itching occurs. Because humans show signs of having the same pathway, the research could pave the way for more effective treatments for conditions such as eczema.

items.title: Revenge motive: Wilson shooting suspect lost friend to gun violence in June
items.summary_raw: The woman shot outside the Wilson courthouse Monday is the sister of Jamel Hines, who was charged with killing LaDarrius Simmons. Simmons was a friend of Abdul Mustafa.

items.title: This Luddite Puppet Hopes You’re Not Reading This on Your Smartphone
items.summary_raw: On this week’s episode of The Big Interview podcast, WIRED’s senior culture editor Manisha Krishnan talks to Gowanus about eschewing Big Tech, going outside, and rejection in the age of dating apps.

items.title: Graham Platner's voters figure out what's next as candidates vie for their support
items.summary_raw: Maine voters are still grappling with Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner's dramatic departure from the race.

items.title: Man killed in ICE shooting was not subject of arrest warrant, Maine Sen. King says
items.summary_raw: Federal and state law enforcement officials are investigating the fatal shooting of a 26-year-old Colombian man by federal law enforcement in Biddeford, Maine, Monday morning.

items.title: Spain end France's bid for 3rd-straight WC final
items.summary_raw: Spain beat France 2-0 in their FIFA World Cup semifinal in Arlington, Texas, on Tuesday to make it three wins in a row against their European heavyweight rival.

items.title: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation
items.summary_raw: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

items.title: Alan Turing's biggest AI assumption may have been wrong
items.summary_raw: A new book claims AI has been built on a flawed assumption dating back to Alan Turing's famous 1950 paper. Peter J. Denning argues that the most important parts of human intelligence, including common sense, intuition, culture, and practical know-how, cannot be encoded into computers. He believes this makes true human-level AI impossible, regardless of how large language models become.

items.title: 4 in Wake County now hospitalized with diarrhea-causing bug as cases jump again
items.summary_raw: Four people in Wake County have been hospitalized by a stomach bug that causes explosive diarrhea that can last for weeks.

items.title: The Chatbot That Foretold Why People Share Secrets With ChatGPT
items.summary_raw: In the 1960s an MIT professor named Joseph Weizenbaum created a chatbot called ELIZA. The conversations people had with it set precedents for the chatbots to come.

items.title: Darline Graham, the sister of Lindsey Graham, is sworn in to serve out his term
items.summary_raw: Darline Graham was sworn in on Tuesday during a brief ceremony on the floor of the Senate. She was appointed to fill the seat of her late brother by South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster.

items.title: Rep. Chellie Pingree on 26-year-old Colombian man killed in ICE shooting in Maine
items.summary_raw: Congresswoman Chellie Pingree, D-Maine, talks about the fatal shooting of a 26-year-old Colombian man by federal law enforcement in Biddeford, Maine.

items.title: Texans co-founder Janice McNair dies at age 89
items.summary_raw: Texans co-founder and senior chair Janice S. McNair died Tuesday in Houston at age 89, the team said.

items.title: Tyrannosaurus rex most expensive dinosaur ever sold
items.summary_raw: The dinosaur skeleton, which roamed the planet 67 million years ago, was sold by Sotheby's in New York.

items.title: New dark matter theory could solve multiple cosmic mysteries at once
items.summary_raw: Dark matter may be far more complicated than scientists once believed. A new study suggests it could consist of at least two different kinds of particles that slowly separate over time, with heavier particles sinking toward the centers of galaxies and lighter ones drifting outward. This simple idea could explain several puzzling cosmic observations that have frustrated astronomers for years, from unusually diffuse dwarf galaxies to surprisingly dense dark matter clumps that bend light through gravitational lensing.

items.title: Falls Lake rises with recent rain
items.summary_raw: According to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Falls Lake's supply pool rose from 62% to 65% after the dam recorded 2.9 inches of rain over the past week.

items.title: Sotheby’s Big T. Rex Auction Raises Concerns Hype and Wealth Are Upending Science
items.summary_raw: Private buyers are increasingly outbidding museums for fossils. That’s making it difficult—or even impossible—for researchers to improve our understanding of the past.

items.title: MLBPA rips salary cap ad campaign as 'perverse'
items.summary_raw: MLB commissioner Rob Manfred and MLBPA head Bruce Meyer shared differing views on the league's "Level the Field" ad campaign expressing its desire for a salary cap.

items.title: Diamond giant De Beers halts work at flagship South African mine as demand plummets
items.summary_raw: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

items.title: Yale scientists found a hidden network inside the eye
items.summary_raw: Researchers have discovered that the retina uses an unexpected communication network that lets separate visual pathways cooperate instead of working alone. A newly identified "commander" cell appears to coordinate this system, helping the eye detect faint details that might otherwise be missed.

items.title: Chatham County asking for help in locating missing man
items.summary_raw: Chatham County Sheriff's office are asking for the public's help in finding Lamar Asim McNeil who last seen Sunday, July 5.

items.title: DOGE Used AI for Housing Policy. The Government Won’t Say How
items.summary_raw: In response to a public records request, HUD has withheld documents about DOGE’s use of AI—in part by citing a privilege that doesn’t exist.

items.title: Police investigation of Jets' Smith no longer acti...
items.summary_raw: An investigation of Geno Smith for an incident in which a woman accused him of battery last month is no longer active, and the New York Jets quarterback will not be charged.
